SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack. Winds 20 -30 mph with stronger gusts.
Comment: A mild and very wet day on Wednesday, although winds are much lighter than they have been recently. Looking ahead, it cools slightly by Friday, with freezing levels around the summits, then goes colder on the weekend, with some snow forecast at higher elevations.
WeatherInfluences: There will be rain to all levels throughout the period, heaviest, and most persistent during the day on Wednesday. Winds will be South-South-West, gale force overnight, but then easing to Fresh to Moderate from Wednesday morning. Freezing level will be well above the summits throughout.
SnowStability:
There will be a significant thaw throughout the day on Wednesday. The existing snowpack is well consolidated, and will become wet, and in general remain stable. Due to rain amounts, some wet snow instability is possible on extremely steep ground above 850 metres on North through East to South aspects giving the possibility of some small wet snow avalanches at these locations.
The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After a mainly dry morning, it became increasingly showery, with rain to all levels by this afternoon. Winds were gale force South-Westerly on the tops and the freezing level was well above the summits throughout.
ObservedSnowStability:
The existing snowpack is thawing and consolidated with some snow loss and depletion of existing snowfields occurring. Greatest snow accumulations can be found above 800 metres, on North through to East aspects. Firmer snow, with icy patches exist at higher elevations.
The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Winds approx 40 - 50 mph making progress very difficult and strenuous.
Comment: Cornice collapse and falling ice from steep ground will be a significant hazard.
WeatherInfluences: The thaw continues. Freezing levels will remain above the tops with rain throughout the day. Fresh to strong winds, Southerly until mid afternoon, then veering South-South-West.
SnowStability: The snowpack will continue to thaw and soften but will remain generally well bonded. Greatest cover will be found on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres. Cornice collapse and falling ice from steep ground will be a significant hazard.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cloudy day with occasional bright spells. Strong gale force winds, South-West at midday but backing to the South by mid afternoon. Around 4 degrees at 900 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw and soften but remains generally well bonded. Greatest snow amounts are found mainly on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 700 metres. Otherwise cover continues to diminish particularly around ridges and sub 700 metres. Cornice collapse and falling ice remain a significant hazard. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack Cloud base was at 700 metres all day. Winds 20 -30 mph progress difficult at times with stronger gusts
Comment: Rockfall heard in the vicinity of the Post Face on Tuesday. Ice and rockfall will remain potential issues during the mild, wet conditions.
WeatherInfluences: Dry for a time very early in the period then wet with rain at all levels. Remaining mild throughout.
SnowStability: Some residual, localised wet snow instability possible on very steep North to East aspects above 950m. Elsewhere, the snowpack will be wet but remain generally well stabilised. Any cornices will further weaken and be prone to collapse. Some limited potential for minor full depth avalanches on selected very steep crags in Coire Ardair.  The avalanche hazard will be Low
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight rainfall eased to occasional light showers of drizzle. Mild at all altitudes.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is moist but generally well bonded in many places. Some localised wet snow instability persists on very steep North to East aspects above 950m. Recent cornice debris noted below a steep North North East aspect in the Inner Coire of Coire Ardair.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate

SnowDistribution: Diminishing snowpack, good above 800 metres. Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Winds approx 40 - 50 mph making progress very difficult and strenuous.
Comment: Turning cooler towards the end of the week.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will remain well above the summits. Southerly gale force winds will decrease overnight to become strong. Dry initially with light rain expected from late morning onwards.
SnowStability: The snowpack will be moist and thawing at all elevations but generally stable throughout. Despite the thaw, snow cover will remain good above 800 metres, particularly in areas that have been sheltered from the wind. Cornices will be weak and prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Predominantly dry with the freezing level well above the summits. Gale force Southerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is moist and slowly thawing at all elevations with generally good stability throughout. Despite the thawing temperatures, cover remains good above 800 metres, particularly in areas that have been sheltered from the wind. Cornices are weak and prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Limited snow cover at lower levels,  best above 800 metres. Poor visibility throughout the day above 800 metres.  Winds 30 mph with stronger gusts.
Comment: Outlook for next 48 hrs. A continuation of the unsettled Westerly airflow bringing further heavy showers and at times longer spells of rain to the West.
WeatherInfluences: It will remain mild with persistent rain at all levels. Winds will be generally Southerly strong overnight, becoming fresh, occasionally strong through the day. The freezing level will remain above the summits.
SnowStability: Mild conditions with rain will further reduce the snow cover. Generally the snowpack will be soft and moist with reasonable stability. Wet snow instabilities will persist above 950 metres in sheltered gullies and on slopes with a North-West through North to Easterly aspect. Gully exits and corrie rims will be most affected. Unstable cornices may also exist in these areas.  The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Mild conditions with rain at all levels have affected the area. Winds have been fresh occasionally strong generally South-Westerly. The freezing level is well above the summits.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw and diminish particularly at lower levels. The remaining snow is soft wet and is generally stable on all but the steepest slopes where wet snow instabilities exist, most affected areas are sheltered gullies and Northwesterly through North to Easterly slopes mainly above 950 metres.  The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack. Poor visibility on the tops.  Winds 30 -40 mph.
Comment: Rock and ice fall a hazard in the mild conditions.
WeatherInfluences: Another mild day with period of rain is expected. The freezing level will remain well above the summits. Winds will be from the South.
SnowStability: The wet snowpack will continue to thaw and consolidate. However, some instabilities will remain on steep North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Cornices above these aspects will be prone to collapse.  Below 1000 metres stability will generally be good. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate
ObservedWeatherInfluences: It was mild throughout with the freezing level above the summits. After some overnight rain the day was generally dry with just of odd light rain shower. Winds were from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is wet throughout and slowly consolidating in the mild conditions. However, wet snow instabilities remain  on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Some substantial cornices remain above these aspects, and are prone to collapse. Below 1000 metres stability is better. The avalanche hazard is Considerable.
